findings.title: Title is very short
findings.recommendation: Expand to ~50–60 characters with the primary query and brand.

findings.title: Meta description may be truncated
findings.evidence: The Verge is about technology and how it makes us feel. Founded in 2011, we offer our audience everything from breaking news to reviews to award-winning features and investigations, on our site, in video, and in podcasts.

findings.title: No H1 heading
findings.recommendation: Add a single H1 that states the page's main topic.

findings.title: Links with no anchor text
findings.recommendation: Give links descriptive anchor text for users and crawlers.

findings.title: Dominant topics detected
findings.detail: jul (46), verge (12), andrew (12), jay (9), peters (9), like (8)  ·  phrases: jay peters, peters jul, andrew webster

findings.title: No visible author/byline
findings.detail: No author meta tag or byline detected near the top.
findings.recommendation: Add a named author with credentials — a core E-E-A-T (experience/expertise) signal.

findings.title: Article-like page without Article schema
findings.detail: Long-form content with no Article/BlogPosting markup.
findings.recommendation: Add Article JSON-LD (headline, author, datePublished) to aid rich results and AI attribution.

findings.title: Few question-based headings
findings.detail: 0 of 6 subheadings are phrased as questions.
findings.recommendation: Rewrite key H2/H3s as the questions users actually ask — AI engines match answers to question-shaped headings.

findings.title: No direct answer up top
findings.detail: The opening doesn't state a concise answer/definition.
findings.recommendation: Lead with a 1–2 sentence direct answer before the details; AI overviews lift the top summary.

findings.title: No llms.txt
findings.recommendation: Optionally publish /llms.txt to guide AI crawlers to your key content. Evidence for ranking impact is still thin — treat as low-cost, low-certainty.

findings.title: AI crawlers are blocked in robots.txt
findings.detail: Disallowed from the site root: ChatGPT-User (OpenAI (ChatGPT on-demand fetch)), ClaudeBot (Anthropic (Claude)), Claude-Web (Anthropic (Claude on-demand)), PerplexityBot (Perplexity), Google-Extended (Google (Gemini / AI Overviews training)), CCBot (Common Crawl (feeds many LLMs)), Bytespider (ByteDance).
findings.recommendation: If you want visibility in AI answers, allow these agents. If the block is intentional (content protection), ignore this.
findings.evidence: ChatGPT-User (OpenAI (ChatGPT on-demand fetch)), ClaudeBot (Anthropic (Claude)), Claude-Web (Anthropic (Claude on-demand)), PerplexityBot (Perplexity), Google-Extended (Google (Gemini / AI Overviews training)), CCBot (Common Crawl (feeds many LLMs)), Bytespider (ByteDance)
